question: List the names of all customers who live in New York.
gold_sql: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE city = 'New York';

question: List the name and price of all products priced above 50.
gold_sql: SELECT name, price FROM products WHERE price > 50;

question: List the name and age of customers aged between 30 and 40 (inclusive).
gold_sql: SELECT name, age FROM customers WHERE age >= 30 AND age <= 40;

question: List the name and price of all products, sorted by price from highest to lowest.
gold_sql: SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price DESC;

question: List the order_id and order_date of all orders with status 'completed'.
gold_sql: SELECT order_id, order_date FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed';

question: List the name and age of the 3 oldest customers.
gold_sql: SELECT name, age FROM customers ORDER BY age DESC LIMIT 3;

question: List the name and price of all products in the 'Sports' category, sorted by price ascending.
gold_sql: SELECT name, price FROM products WHERE category = 'Sports' ORDER BY price ASC;

question: List the name and city of all female customers.
gold_sql: SELECT name, city FROM customers WHERE gender = 'F';

question: Find the name and price of the cheapest product.
gold_sql: SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price ASC LIMIT 1;

question: List the order_id and order_date of all orders placed on or after 2024-03-01.
gold_sql: SELECT order_id, order_date FROM orders WHERE order_date >= '2024-03-01';

question: For each customer who placed at least one order, list their name and the number of orders they placed.
gold_sql: SELECT c.name, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name;

question: For each product category, show the category, the number of products, and the average price.
gold_sql: SELECT category, COUNT(*) AS product_count, AVG(price) AS avg_price FROM products GROUP BY category;

question: List each city and the number of customers living in it.
gold_sql: SELECT city, COUNT(customer_id) AS customer_count FROM customers GROUP BY city;

question: List the cities that have more than one customer.
gold_sql: SELECT city, COUNT(customer_id) AS customer_count FROM customers GROUP BY city HAVING COUNT(customer_id) > 1;

question: For each order, show the order_id, order_date, and the total amount (sum of quantity * price).
gold_sql: SELECT o.order_id, o.order_date, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.price) AS total_amount FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY o.order_id, o.order_date;

question: Count the number of orders in each status.
gold_sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders GROUP BY status;

question: For each product, show the product name and the total quantity sold across all orders.
gold_sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.product_id, p.name;

question: List the product categories whose average price is above 50.
gold_sql: SELECT category, AVG(price) AS average_price FROM products GROUP BY category HAVING AVG(price) > 50;

question: For each customer, show their name and the total amount they have spent.
gold_sql: SELECT c.name, SUM(oi.price * oi.quantity) AS total_amount FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name;

question: Which customer placed the most orders? Show their name and order count.
gold_sql: SELECT c.name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name ORDER BY COUNT(o.order_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
note: Known tie: Alice Chen and Bob Smith each have 2 orders; LIMIT 1 returns one arbitrarily. Harness should treat tied top-N rows as acceptable alternatives.
